titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.7

div.p: Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting. Know that I have conceded to the monks of Melrose that they may have their own land of Mow for ever in free forest. Wherefore we strictly forbid that anyone should fell timber or hunt without their permission, on pain of our full forfeiture of ten pounds. As witnesses: Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian; Roger Avenel; David the Marischal. At Edinburgh, on the twenty-eighth day of May, in the twenty-second year of the reign of the lord king.
